factors.name: Fed Policy Shift (Warsh tenure start)
factors.summary: Fed Chair Warsh held rates at 3.5-3.75% but stripped cut-bias language, removed dot plot, and signaled potential rate hikes this year. This hawkish surprise caused initial equity sell-off; markets are still repricing guardrail removal and policy opacity.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Hormuz reopening)
factors.summary: US-Iran MoU signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening with 60-day suspension of transit charges. Oil retreating sharply ($60-65 target by Q1'27 per Citi), easing inflation concerns and supporting equity valuations. Risk premium lifting supports risk appetite.

factors.name: USD Strength (DXY near 100.80, 1-year high)
factors.summary: Dollar surged to highest level since May 2025 post-Fed hawkish hold. Strong USD pressures multinational earnings translation for Dow (50% international exposure); headwind for CAT, BA, MSFT, GE and other exporters.

factors.name: Japan BoJ Hawkish Messaging
factors.summary: BoJ Deputy Governor Himino explicitly tied FX moves to inflation impact; BoJ minutes show near-unanimous recognition underlying inflation approaching 2%, keeping further hikes on table. USD/JPY spike to 161.80 then sharp reversal suggests intervention risk, creating FX volatility headwind.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Long-end yields under pressure but front-end held firm on hawkish Fed surprise. Equity rate-sensitive segments (utilities, REITs, dividend payers in Dow) face headwind from sticky real yields and surprise cut-bias removal.

factors.name: Equity Market Internals (yesterday's post-Fed reaction)
factors.summary: Russell 2000 +2.12% and NASDAQ +1.91% on 18th, but Dow lagged at +0.14% — index composition bias against rate-sensitive, dividend-heavy industrials. Current 4h/24h declines (-0.28%/-0.26%) confirm Dow underperformance persists into 19th.

factors.name: Oil & Commodity Price Action
factors.summary: Oil set for deep weekly loss as Hormuz reopens; gold also down (weekly loss, hawkish Fed outweighing peace premium). Lower energy and commodity costs reduce input inflation for Dow industrials and lower transport costs, supporting margins.

factors.name: Cyclical Forward Guidance Risk
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings or guidance releases in immediate 24h window; earnings season backdrop mixed. Accenture guidance miss noted, but most Dow heavies (UNH, MSFT, GS, CAT, BA, JPM) not reporting imminently.

factors.name: China/Asia Macro Backdrop
factors.summary: Hong Kong and mainland China markets closed (Juneteenth holiday spillover + local holiday). Thin Asia trading limits real-time feedback on China PMI, commodity demand from CAT/DE end-markets. Japan May CPI in-line, not a catalyst.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Unwind
factors.summary: Iran deal removes tail risk of further Strait disruption; removes war-premium from oil and equities. Supports sentiment for risk-on unwind, but Dow's value/dividend nature means this benefits growth/small-cap more than Dow heavies.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between two opposing forces: (1) hawkish Fed surprise (Warsh's opacity and rate-hike guidance) pressuring valuations and the dollar surging to 1-year highs, which undercuts multinational earnings translation; (2) Iran peace deal de-risking inflation and reopening Hormuz, lowering oil/commodity costs and easing input-cost pressure on industrials. Dow's 4h/-0.28% and 24h/-0.26% declines reflect index-heavy weighting toward rate-sensitive financials and dividend payers, which are most exposed to sticky real yields and the surprise removal of Fed cut guidance. Russell 2000 and NASDAQ's relative strength (+2.12%, +1.91% yesterday) suggests growth and small-cap are trading the Hormuz relief; Dow is lagging because of its dividend+financial composition. Markets are still repricing the new Fed regime (less talk, more opacity) and FX volatility (BoJ rhetoric, USD strength, JPY intervention risk) is adding noise.
catalysts_ahead: US markets CLOSED Friday 19 June (Juneteenth holiday) — no US cash equity/bond trading
Next major US data: Durable goods orders, factory orders (early July window)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (early July) — critical Dow cyclical indicator
Fed speak from Warsh or other governors (ongoing, high attention on policy communication style shift)
Dow component earnings: UNH, JPM, GS, CAT, BA guidance (late June/early July)
China macro data (PMI, production) — CAT, DE, MA demand driver (delayed by holiday closures)
10-year Treasury yield stability test — if yields spike further, equities at risk; if stable/lower, supports rebound
BoJ next meeting or guidance (late June/early July) — yen intervention and rate normalization path
screening.risk_warning: Bias will flip BULLISH if: (1) 10-year yields break lower and real yields compress (signaling Fed cut expectations return); (2) Dow component earnings (especially CAT, GE, HON) beat and raise, eroding bearish guidance narrative; (3) DXY rolls over sharply post-holiday, reducing FX headwind. Bias will flip BEARISH if: (1) Iran deal unravels (Hormuz re-closes); (2) BoJ hawkish pivot accelerates yen strength (JPY >162 USD/JPY), pressuring global equities; (3) Warsh signals no near-term hikes and cuts guidance (dovish surprise). Market is currently in repricing chop; no clear directional conviction until either Fed policy communication settles or Dow component earnings provide earnings-growth refresh.
screening.entry_guidance: Move partially underway (Dow -0.28% in 4h post-Iran deal and hawkish Fed surprise); wait for Juneteenth holiday close and retest of 51400–51500 support before considering tactical long. Short conviction low given offsetting Hormuz relief; avoid size until either Fed repricing settles or next earnings catalyst.
